---
title: "Reusable Nested Structures"
subtitle: "Build once, instantiate repeatedly, and use nested paths"
jupyter: python3
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  error: false
---

This tutorial builds a diblock, reuses it to make a two-arm star, then reuses
the star to make `starChain`. It assumes the Python environment from the
[diblock tutorial](diblock-copolymer.qmd).

## Construction levels

```text
Level 1: polyA -- polyB                 diblock
Level 2: (polyA -- polyB) x 2           star
Level 3: star1 = star2                   starChain
```

Colons descend into structures. For example,
`star1:diblock2:polyB.end2` addresses `polyB.end2` inside `diblock2` inside
`star1`.

## Python

```{python}
import pyseb

world = pyseb.World("NestedTutorial")

diblock = world.Add("GaussianPolymer", "polyA")
world.Link("GaussianPolymer", "polyB.end1", "polyA.end2")

star = world.Add(diblock, "diblock1")
world.Link(diblock, "diblock2:polyA.end1", "diblock1:polyA.end1")

chain = world.Add(star, "star1")
world.Link(
    star,
    "star2:diblock1:polyB.end2",
    "star1:diblock2:polyB.end2",
)
world.Add(chain, "starChain")

parameters = {
    "beta_polyA": 1.0,
    "beta_polyB": 0.75,
    "Rg_polyA": 5.0,
    "Rg_polyB": 8.0,
}
q = [0.01, 0.03, 0.1, 0.3]
values = world.EvaluateFormFactor("starChain", parameters, q)

for q_value, value in zip(q, values):
    print(f"q={q_value:g}, F(q)={value:.8f}")
```

The eight polymer instances reuse four physical parameters because their
internal subunit names are shared.

## C++

```cpp
{{< include cpp/nested-structures.cpp >}}
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
from _helpers import run_tutorial

run_tutorial("nested-structures")
```

## Next steps

- [Sweep model parameters](parameter-sweeps.qmd)
- [Core concepts](https://github.com/Tobionecenobi/pySEB/wiki/Core-Concepts)
- [Naming and linking](https://github.com/Tobionecenobi/pySEB/wiki/Naming-and-Linking)
